title: veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. anagalloides
intro: Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. anagalloides, commonly known as the aquatic speedwell, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The aquatic speedwell is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and treating skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
